name: Mercosur
climate: Tropical in the north, subtropical in central regions, temperate and arid in the south, Amazonian rainforest in northwest Brazil

neighbors.name: USMCA
neighbors.relevance: US is a major trade partner; agricultural and energy exports flow north; tariff policies affect Mercosur competitiveness

neighbors.name: European Union
neighbors.relevance: EU-Mercosur trade deal (agreed 2019, ratification ongoing) would create one of the world's largest free trade zones

neighbors.name: BRICS
neighbors.relevance: Brazil and Argentina are BRICS members; South-South trade and investment growing

neighbors.name: CPTPP
neighbors.relevance: Chile (associate member) is in CPTPP; Mercosur countries exploring Pacific trade links

neighbors.name: Cape of Good Hope Sea Route
neighbors.relevance: South Atlantic shipping connects Brazilian ports to southern Africa and Asia via the Cape

links.description: EU-Mercosur agreement covers beef, auto parts, and industrial goods; decades of negotiation
US is Brazil's second-largest trade partner; soybeans, steel, and aircraft are key exports
Brazil is a founding BRICS member; Argentina joined in 2024; alternative development finance via NDB
Brazil and Argentina are G20 members; Brazil hosted 2024 G20 summit
Brazilian and Argentine grain exports transit the Panama Canal to reach Asian markets
Iron ore and soybean shipments from Brazil to China often route via the Cape
